author: misilva73
content:
I agree with Gary's proposal. We should move this to "charge-at-access", i.e.,  we move the CALL*/CREATE account-creation charge into the post-state phase at the access point, matching lines the SELFDESTRUCT mechanism. The fail-before-access refund logic disappears (no charge occurs); frame-revert refunds stay on the unified rule.

How are specs and implementations doing it atm? Are they aligned with the 7929 or 8037?

Happy to bring it up on ACDT today

author: chfast
content: Seems none of the implementations is respecting "After an exceptional halt, state_gas_reservoir is reset back to its value at the start of the child frame", or I don't understand it. I read it as: in case of failure other than REVERT, we don't apply gas spilling quirks. This way it make more sense to me. <@968526635735326741> <@402853454126776320> <@429719902363058177> <@360491619402776577> <@954738828445372416> <@755913785620824175>

author: misilva73
content: As an example, if we have gas_left=5 and reservoir=10 in the call frame at the start and the call reverts, I would expect the final accounting to be gas_left=0 and reservoir=10. Is this not how it is being implemented?

author: chfast
content: it can also end with gas_left=0, reservoir=12 if there was 2-gas spill inside the inner call.

author: misilva73
content: This would lead the issue we discussed during interop where depending on where the revert happens inside nested call, the final accounting can be different. It needs to be gas_left=0 and reservoir=10.

author: misilva73
content: Here is my report on this: https://github.com/misilva73/evm-gas-repricings/blob/main/reports/eip-8037/eip-8037-gas-accounting-cases.md

author: misilva73
content: Cases A and C have different gas costs, which is a problem.

author: chfast
content: By the same paragraph in the spec, case A should end with (0, 5) (reset reservoir to the value you started with).
